meta.name: tradingview-mcp-thaqif
meta.author: Thaqif
meta.description: Improved TradingView MCP rules for Pine Script v5/v6 development, FCPO/futures trading indicators, and AI-assisted trading systems.
meta.rule_format: Each rule is an object: { id, rule, rationale, severity, example?, anti_example? }. Severity is one of: critical, high, medium, low. JSON has no native comments, so rationale/example fields carry the explanation.
meta.how_to_use: Load this file as the project rules/context for an AI coding assistant (Claude + TradingView MCP). Treat 'critical' and 'high' rules as hard constraints during Pine Script generation and review.

primary_instruments: FCPO — Bursa Malaysia Crude Palm Oil Futures (TradingView: MYX:FCPO1! continuous front month; 25 metric tons/contract; tick = RM1/ton = RM25/contract; quoted in MYR per metric ton)
BTCUSDT — retained from v1 for the scalper demo profile
methodology: Wyckoff (accumulation, markup, distribution, markdown; events PS/SC/AR/ST/Spring/SOS/LPS/UTAD/SOW/LPSY)
Multi-timeframe top-down (Daily bias -> H4 structure -> H1 zone -> M15 trigger)
Effort vs Result via volume confirmation
fcpo_external_drivers: CBOT soybean oil ZL/BO (substitute edible oil)
MPOB monthly data (production, exports, closing stocks/inventory)
SGS / AmSpec / ITS export cargo surveys
fcpo_sessions_myt_gmt8: Lunch break 12:30-14:30 (no trading; beware gap on reopen)

pine_script.description: Core Pine Script v5/v6 coding standards. These govern how indicators, strategies, and libraries are written so they compile cleanly, do not repaint, and stay maintainable.

rules.rule: Declare the Pine version on the very first line with //@version=6 (use //@version=5 only when a dependency forces it).
rules.rationale: The version annotation must precede all code or the script fails to compile. v6 fixes type/na inconsistencies and allows request.* in local scopes.

rules.rule: Use exactly one declaration statement (indicator(), strategy(), or library()) and set overlay explicitly.
rules.rationale: Implicit overlay leads to plots landing in the wrong pane. Explicit overlay=true/false documents intent.

rules.rule: Always use namespaced built-ins: ta.*, math.*, str.*, array.*, matrix.*, request.*, input.*, color.*, strategy.*.
rules.rationale: Un-namespaced v3/v4 calls (sma, rsi, security) are removed in v5/v6 and break compilation.

rules.rule: Define all user inputs through input.* (input.int, input.float, input.bool, input.source, input.timeframe, input.session, input.string) with title, defval, and group/tooltip.
rules.rationale: Typed inputs give validation, grouped settings UI, and self-documenting tooltips.
rules.example: len = input.int(14, "RSI Length", minval=2, group="Momentum", tooltip="Lookback for RSI")

rules.rule: Initialize state that must persist across bars with var (or varip only for intrabar counters), never by reassigning a fresh value every bar.
rules.rationale: var initializes once; plain assignment resets each bar and loses accumulated state.

rules.rule: Guard division, sqrt, log, and array access against na and zero; prefer nz() and explicit bounds checks.
rules.rationale: na or divide-by-zero silently produces na series that propagate and blank out plots.

rules.rule: Declare max_bars_back, max_lines_count, max_labels_count, and max_boxes_count when referencing deep history or drawing many objects.
rules.rationale: Default limits (~50 drawings, dynamic bars_back) cause runtime errors or dropped drawings on busy charts.

rules.rule: Keep per-bar work O(1) where possible; precompute series once and avoid recomputing inside for/while loops on every bar.
rules.rationale: Heavy loops each bar hit the execution-time limit and slow the chart.

rules.rule: Use request.security() for higher-timeframe data with lookahead=barmerge.lookahead_off and gaps=barmerge.gaps_off, and never read the live HTF bar for a signal.
rules.rationale: Reading an unconfirmed HTF bar is the #1 cause of repainting.

rules.rule: Comment non-obvious logic with single-line // comments, and structure files top-down: version, declaration, inputs, calculations, signals, plots/alerts.
rules.rationale: A consistent layout makes scripts reviewable and diffable across versions.

rules.rule: Prefer if/switch expression-assignment (v6) over nested ternaries longer than two levels.
rules.rationale: v6 lets if/switch return values, which is far more readable than chained ?: and avoids precedence bugs.

rules.rule: Plot with explicit titles and display arguments (plot, plotshape, plotchar, hline, bgcolor, fill, label.new, line.new, box.new); never leave plots untitled.
rules.rationale: Titled plots are addressable by the MCP data_get_study_values tool and by alerts/exports.

indicators.description: Standards for indicator selection, calculation, and combination. Avoid redundant, correlated signals and prefer volume/volatility-aware confirmation.

rules.rule: Use RSI(14) as a momentum filter, not a standalone trigger; only act when RSI is between 30 and 70 unless the strategy explicitly trades exhaustion.
rules.rationale: Carried over and formalized from v1 — extremes are mean-reversion zones, not trend-continuation entries.

rules.rule: Confirm price moves with volume (Effort vs Result): rising price on rising volume = valid; rising price on falling volume = suspect.
rules.rationale: Core Wyckoff law; divergence between effort (volume) and result (price) flags weakness.

rules.rule: Use ATR (14) for volatility-normalized stops and targets instead of fixed point distances.
rules.rationale: FCPO and crude volatility shifts by session and regime; fixed stops are too tight in expansion and too wide in contraction.

rules.rule: Anchor VWAP to the session for intraday FCPO/crude work using ta.vwap with a session-reset condition.
rules.rationale: Session-anchored VWAP marks fair value for the active session and aligns with composite-man accumulation/distribution.

rules.rule: Do not stack multiple highly-correlated momentum oscillators (e.g., RSI + Stoch + CCI) as independent confirmations.
rules.rationale: Correlated indicators create false confidence; they confirm each other, not the market.

rules.rule: When detecting divergence, compare confirmed pivots (ta.pivothigh/ta.pivotlow) not raw highs/lows, and require pivot confirmation before signaling.
rules.rationale: Unconfirmed pivots repaint as new bars form.

rules.rule: Expose every signal-relevant value as a titled plot or a plot(display=display.data_window) so the MCP can read it via data_get_study_values.
rules.rationale: AI-assisted review depends on machine-readable indicator output.

multi_timeframe.description: Top-down multi-timeframe alignment rules (Daily -> H4 -> H1 -> M15) with non-repainting HTF retrieval.

rules.rule: Establish directional bias on the higher timeframe first (Daily/H4), then refine structure on H1, then time entries on M15. Do not take M15 signals against the Daily/H4 bias.
rules.rationale: Trade only in the direction of least resistance (Wyckoff). Counter-trend M15 entries fight the dominant force.

rules.rule: Retrieve non-repainting HTF values with the confirmed-bar pattern: request.security(sym, htf, expr[1], lookahead=barmerge.lookahead_on).
rules.rationale: Using expr[1] with lookahead_on returns the last CLOSED HTF value on every chart bar — no future leak, no repaint.

rules.rule: Use input.timeframe() for HTF parameters and validate that the requested timeframe is higher than the chart timeframe (timeframe.in_seconds()).
rules.rationale: Requesting a lower TF than the chart can introduce intrabar lookahead and confusing results.

rules.rule: Place entries at Fibonacci confluence (0.382/0.5/0.618/0.705/0.786) of the HTF impulse leg, prioritizing zones where a Fib level overlaps a Wyckoff event (Spring/LPS or UTAD/LPSY).
rules.rationale: Confluence of structure + Fib + volume raises the probability of the zone holding.

rules.rule: For intrabar detail use request.security_lower_tf() rather than guessing intrabar movement from the chart timeframe.
rules.rationale: security_lower_tf returns an array of lower-TF values per bar, the supported way to inspect intrabar action.

wyckoff.description: Codifying Wyckoff method analysis into Pine Script: phase identification, event labeling, and composite-man logic. Distribusi = Distribution (bearish); Akumulasi = Accumulation (bullish).

rules.rule: Label the four phases explicitly (Accumulation, Markup, Distribution, Markdown) and never enter during the range/cause phase — wait for the breakout/breakdown (effect).
rules.rationale: Law of Cause and Effect: the size of the range determines the move; entering mid-range has poor R:R.

rules.rule: Detect a Spring as a false break below range support that closes back inside on a wide-spread bar, ideally with a volume spike, and treat it as a primary long trigger.
rules.rationale: Spring traps bears and marks Last Point of Support territory — a best-quality long per the methodology.
rules.example: spring = low < support and close > support and ta.highest(volume,20) == volume

rules.rule: Detect a UTAD (Upthrust After Distribution) as a false break above range resistance that closes back inside, and treat it as a primary short trigger.
rules.rationale: UTAD traps bulls at the top of distribution — the mirror of a Spring.

rules.rule: Classify climactic bars (SC/BC) as wide-spread + very high volume + close in the lower/upper third; require an Automatic Rally/Reaction (AR) before assuming a range has formed.
rules.rationale: A single climax is not a range; the AR defines the opposite boundary.

rules.rule: Apply the price-volume truth table in signal logic: UP+VolUp=strong demand; UP+VolDown=weak rally; DOWN+VolUp=strong supply; DOWN+VolDown=weak selling; narrow spread + high vol = absorption/churn.
rules.rationale: This table is the operational core of Effort vs Result and should gate every breakout signal.

rules.rule: Frame every setup with the composite-man question: what is smart money doing here, and where are retail traders trapped?
rules.rationale: Keeps logic aligned with accumulation-at-lows / distribution-at-highs rather than chasing.

rules.rule: Set Wyckoff-consistent stops: for longs, below the Spring low; for shorts, above the UTAD high.
rules.rationale: These are the invalidation points where the trap thesis is proven wrong.

fcpo.description: FCPO / crude-oil specific development patterns: session gating, contract maths, and inter-market context.

rules.rule: Gate intraday signals to liquid FCPO sessions using input.session and time(); suppress signals during the pre-open auction, the 12:30-14:30 lunch break, and the first 1-2 bars after the afternoon reopen.
rules.rationale: Auction and reopen bars are thin and gap-prone, producing false breakouts.

rules.rule: Compute FCPO P&L with the real contract spec: 25 metric tons/contract, tick = RM1/ton = RM25/contract. Convert price-distance stops to ringgit risk before sizing.
rules.rationale: Position sizing in lots is wrong unless tick value is modeled; 1 point move = RM25 per lot.
rules.example: riskRM = math.abs(entry - stop) * 25  // RM per 1 lot

rules.rule: Treat Dalian palm olein and CBOT soybean oil (ZL) as lead/context markets; when building bias indicators, allow an optional request.security() pull of a correlated symbol for confirmation.
rules.rationale: FCPO frequently follows the Asian Dalian session and tracks the broader edible-oil complex.

rules.rule: Flag MPOB report days and major export-survey releases (SGS/AmSpec/ITS) as elevated-volatility windows; widen stops or stand aside around them via a date/session input.
rules.rationale: Inventory and export prints drive gap risk that mechanical stops cannot absorb.

rules.rule: Incorporate USD/MYR direction as a slow macro filter for multi-day FCPO bias (stronger USD/MYR generally supports export demand).
rules.rationale: Currency competitiveness is a persistent driver of palm export flows.

rules.rule: Account for the lunch-break gap: do not carry an intrabar stop assuming continuous fills across 12:30-14:30, and re-evaluate structure on the reopen.
rules.rationale: FCPO is not continuous; the midday gap can jump a resting stop.

backtesting.description: Rules for building honest, robust strategy backtests in Pine Script that survive out-of-sample and live trading.

rules.rule: Set process_orders_on_close=true (or fill on the next bar open) so signals do not fill at the same bar they are computed on.
rules.rationale: Same-bar fills assume you acted before the bar closed — a classic look-ahead inflation of results.

rules.rule: Model realistic costs: set commission_type/commission_value and slippage to your broker's FCPO round-turn brokerage and typical slippage; never backtest at zero cost.
rules.rationale: Scalping and high-frequency edges usually vanish once real costs are applied.

rules.rule: Keep calc_on_every_tick=false for historical backtests; only enable it knowingly for live/forward simulation, and document that results will differ.
rules.rationale: calc_on_every_tick changes intrabar evaluation and can repaint signals between history and realtime.

rules.rule: Require a minimum sample (target 100+ trades) before trusting metrics, and report trade count alongside net profit, profit factor, max drawdown, and average R.
rules.rationale: Small samples overfit; headline net profit without drawdown and trade count is meaningless.

rules.rule: Validate out-of-sample: reserve a hold-out period or use walk-forward, and reject parameter sets that only work on the in-sample window.
rules.rationale: Curve-fitting to one window is the most common reason live results diverge from backtests.

rules.rule: Use the FCPO contract multiplier (RM25/point) in default_qty / pyramiding settings so reported P&L is in real ringgit, and cap pyramiding deliberately.
rules.rationale: Default qty in contracts with the wrong multiplier misstates risk and reward.

rules.rule: Constrain backtests to tradable sessions and exclude illiquid auction bars to avoid fills that are not achievable live.
rules.rationale: Backtests that fill in non-tradable windows overstate realizable edge.

alerts.description: Rules for building reliable, automation-ready alerts (including JSON payloads for webhook/MCP-driven workflows).

rules.rule: Fire alerts only on confirmed bars: gate alert()/strategy alerts with barstate.isconfirmed or use alert.freq_once_per_bar_close.
rules.rationale: Intrabar alerts repaint and produce duplicate/false notifications.
rules.example:
if longSignal and barstate.isconfirmed
    alert("FCPO LONG", alert.freq_once_per_bar_close)

rules.rule: Build alert messages as structured strings that include symbol, timeframe, side, price, and reason; for automation use a JSON payload with str.tostring().
rules.rationale: Downstream MCP/webhook consumers need machine-parseable context, not free text.

rules.rule: Use alertcondition() for simple static indicator alerts and the alert() function for dynamic, data-driven messages; do not mix both for the same event.
rules.rationale: alertcondition has a fixed message; alert() allows runtime content. Mixing duplicates alerts.

rules.rule: De-duplicate: track the last alerted bar/condition in a var and suppress repeats while the condition stays true.
rules.rationale: Prevents alert storms when a condition remains true across bars.

rules.rule: Never place sensitive credentials or webhook secrets inside Pine alert messages.
rules.rationale: Alert text can be logged and shared; secrets belong in the webhook receiver, not the script.

risk_management.description: Position sizing and capital-protection rules, generalized from the v1 scalper risk block and adapted for FCPO/futures.

rules.rule: Risk a fixed fraction of equity per trade (default 1%); derive lot size from (equity * risk%) / (stop-distance * tick value), not from a fixed lot count.
rules.rationale: Constant fractional risk normalizes exposure across volatility regimes.

rules.rule: Always define stop and target before entry; reject any setup whose reward:risk is below the configured minimum (default 1.5R).
rules.rationale: Edge requires positive expectancy; sub-threshold R:R erodes it even at high win rate.

rules.rule: Enforce a daily loss limit and a max-trades-per-session cap (v1 used max 6 trades/run); halt new entries once hit.
rules.rationale: Caps tilt and limits damage during adverse regimes.

rules.rule: Place stops at structural invalidation (below Spring/support for longs, above UTAD/resistance for shorts), then size to that distance — never widen the stop to fit a desired size.
rules.rationale: Stops follow structure; size follows stops. Reversing this is how accounts blow up.

rules.rule: For time-based scalps (v1 model), treat the time exit as the stop and still cap per-trade and per-run notional explicitly.
rules.rationale: A time-based exit without a notional cap leaves tail risk on gaps.

mcp_tools.description: Best practices for driving the TradingView MCP (read/control a live chart, develop Pine Script, manage alerts) from an AI assistant. Tool names refer to the tradingview MCP server.

rules.rule: Call chart_get_state first in any session to learn the current symbol, timeframe, and the names + entity IDs of visible indicators before acting.
rules.rationale: Most other tools need the symbol/timeframe/indicator context; acting blind causes wrong-target edits.

rules.rule: Use full indicator names with chart_manage_indicator (e.g., "Relative Strength Index", not "RSI"; "Moving Average Exponential", not "EMA").
rules.rationale: The MCP matches official study names; abbreviations fail to resolve.

rules.rule: Always pass summary=true to data_get_ohlcv unless individual bars are strictly required.
rules.rationale: Full bar dumps are large and slow; summaries answer most questions.

rules.rule: Read live indicator values with data_get_study_values; read custom Pine drawings with data_get_pine_lines/labels/tables/boxes and always pass study_filter to target one indicator by name.
rules.rationale: Without study_filter the tools return everything and are ambiguous; the indicator must also be visible on the chart.

rules.rule: After pine_set_source, run pine_smart_compile, then pine_get_errors (and pine_get_console if needed) before relying on the script.
rules.rationale: Inject -> compile -> check-errors is the only safe edit loop; silent compile failures otherwise go unnoticed.

rules.rule: Avoid pine_get_source on large/complex scripts unless actually editing; it can return 200KB+ and waste context.
rules.rationale: The MCP guidance warns complex scripts return very large source.

rules.rule: When verifying an indicator visually, use capture_screenshot with an explicit region (full, chart, or strategy_tester).
rules.rationale: Targeted regions are faster and clearer than full captures for review.

rules.rule: Test signal logic with the replay tools (replay_start -> replay_step -> replay_status -> replay_stop) before trusting it on live data; use batch_run to sweep multiple symbols/timeframes.
rules.rationale: Replay reproduces bar-by-bar evaluation and surfaces repainting; batch_run scales validation.

rules.rule: Treat alert_create/alert_list/alert_delete as the alert lifecycle; list before create to avoid duplicates and delete stale alerts.
rules.rationale: Orphaned alerts fire unexpectedly and clutter automation.

rules.rule: Do not auto-execute live orders or move funds from MCP/automation; surface signals and let the human place the trade.
rules.rationale: Safety: AI-driven execution on a live brokerage is out of scope and high-risk; the v1 project is explicitly a demo.

strategy_profiles.description: Named, reusable strategy configurations. The original v1 scalper is preserved here verbatim in intent so nothing from the source is lost.

ten_second_momentum_scalper.name: 10-Second Momentum Scalper (v1 legacy, demo only)
sources: Price momentum — buy if last close > previous close (green candle)
RSI filter — only trade when RSI is not at an extreme (30-70 range)

long: Last close > previous close by at least 0.05%
short: Last close < previous close by at least 0.05%

exit_rules: Exit automatically after 10 seconds (next loop tick closes previous position)
No manual exit management — pure time-based scalp
risk_rules: Fixed trade size: 1% of portfolio per trade
No stop loss — exit is time-based at next tick
Demo purposes only — minimum exchange order size may apply
ten_second_momentum_scalper.notes: Demo strategy for a YouTube video. Runs 6 trades over 60 seconds, one every 10 seconds. Retained for reference; superseded by the Wyckoff/MTF FCPO methodology above for real analysis.
